detail:
I now have a complete picture. No code was changed (read/run/analyze only). Here is the structured exploration summary.

---

# Explorer summary вАФ `mcp` (TOTP-gated `save_note` MCP server)

## Verdict
This is a **greenfield feature**, not a bug hunt вАФ there is no existing `note_server/`, no `mcp` dependency, and no prior TOTP/MCP code anywhere in the tree. The spec is unusually precise, so the real risk is **not** the server logic (which is straightforward) вАФ it's the **repo's authoritative merge gate**, which does far more than the spec's AC8 admits (`ruff` + `basedpyright` + `pytest` + **diff-cover + semgrep + gitleaks**). Three of those interact badly with this task's requirements unless handled deliberately. Those are the findings below.

## Key facts established
- **MCP SDK API** (official `mcp` package, `mcp.server.fastmcp.FastMCP`): register with `@mcp.tool()`; a signature `def save_note(token: str, project_name: str, content: str) -> str` auto-generates an input schema with all three required string params (satisfies AC1). Bind host/port are constructor settings: `FastMCP(name, host=..., port=...)`; start with `mcp.run(transport="streamable-http")`. **Default Streamable-HTTP mount path is `/mcp`** вЖТ the public address is `https://<vps-host>:8443/mcp` (proxy вЖТ internal bind). ([running-server docs](https://gofastmcp.com/deployment/running-server), [python-sdk](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/python-sdk))
- **The base32 secret decodes cleanly.** `KNVWKZLWFVHWW2LOMF3WC` (len 21) вЖТ pad to 24 with `===` вЖТ `base64.b32decode` вЖТ **13 bytes** (ASCII `Skeev-Okinawa`). Reference TOTP vectors (HMAC-SHA1, 6 digits, 30s) I computed for the developer to assert against:
  - `t=0` (counter 0) вЖТ **`411400`**
  - `t=59` (counter 1) вЖТ **`795119`**
  - `t=1234567890` (counter 41152263) вЖТ **`948172`**

## Affected files (proposed)
| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `note_server/__init__.py`, `config.py`, `totp.py`, `writer.py`, `server.py` | new package (see approach) |
| `pyp

All artifacts (`__pycache__`, `.venv`) are gitignored. The task is complete.

## Summary

I built `note_server/`, a self-contained TOTP-gated MCP server exposing exactly one `save_note` tool, plus its test suite вАФ a greenfield feature (no prior MCP/TOTP code existed). The package is split so every security-relevant piece is unit-testable without standing up HTTP: `totp.py` (RFC 6238 HMAC-SHA1/6-digit/30s verifier with an **injectable clock** and a ¬±1-step drift window, hardcoded base32 secret decoding to `Skeev-Okinawa`), `writer.py` (the `^[A-Za-z0-9_-]+$` allowlist path-traversal guard + a TOCTOU-free `O_CREAT|O_EXCL` no-clobber writer that dedups to `-2.md`/`-3.md`вА¶ with a defence-in-depth "stays inside the folder" check), `config.py` (`NoteConfig.from_env`, all three env vars required with no default, mirroring the repo's `ConfigError` idiom), and `server.py` (the pure `handle_save_note` orchestrator вАФ authвЖТvalidateвЖТwrite, returning check-naming error strings that never leak the folder path вАФ wrapped in a `FastMCP` `@tool` whose thin transport layer supplies `now=time.time()`). A `README.md` documents the env vars and the "TLS proxy terminates HTTPS on 8443 вЖТ forwards to the plain-HTTP bind at `/mcp`" contract.

**Tests** (`tests/note_server/`, 50 passing) cover every acceptance criterion: tool surface via `list_tools` (AC1), TOTP accept/¬±1/reject-¬±2/wrong/non-ASCII with reference-vector assertions and no-write-on-reject (AC2), each `project_name` rejection incl. explicit traversal cases writing nothing (AC3), verbatim happy-path write (AC4), pre-planted dedup leaving the original untouched (AC5), path-never-leaks assertions (AC6), config success + missing-folder/host/port + bad-port failures (AC7), plus failure-mode tests per function (pre-epoch timestamp, non-base32 secret, dedup-limit exhaustion) and an end-to-end `call_tool` round-trip. I addressed the three gate-interaction risks the explorer flagged: `mcp` is declared in `pyproject.toml` `[project].depende
